headlines.title: Bayeux Tapestry arrives in UK for first time in 900 years under police guard
headlines.description: The famous artefact depicting events leading up to the Norman Conquest of England will go on display in September.

headlines.title: Former Tory minister Ann Widdecombe dies at 78
headlines.description: Ann Widdecombe became a dominant pro-Brexit voice later in her career.

headlines.title: Police investigating Reform donations - reports
headlines.description: The investigation is looking into donations made to Reform UK by the mother of the convicted fraudster George Cottrell, according to the Times.

headlines.title: 'Cool in 90 seconds' - the fake portable air conditioners sweeping the internet
headlines.description: The ASA says adverts claiming small devices can rapidly cool rooms were too good to be true.

headlines.title: Pioneering treatment saves identical twins from rare pregnancy condition
headlines.description: Identical twins Nancy and Margo benefitted from the procedure while in the womb as part of a world-first medical trial.

headlines.title: To Wimbledon and back - Fery's voyage to Centre Court spotlight
headlines.description: Arthur Fery learned how to play tennis about a mile away from Wimbledon's Centre Court - now he's one win from the SW19 final.

headlines.title: Ukrainian agent accused of murdering Monaco bomb suspect changes story
headlines.description: Two men with links to Ukraine's security services are accused of killing Anastasiia Berezovska and dumping her body in woods.

headlines.title: Prince Harry plays pickleball at Invictus Games event, but no Meghan
headlines.description: The Duke of Sussex is travelling without his wife for the event at Birmingham's NEC.

headlines.title: Thousands of offenders not wearing electronic tags, report says
headlines.description: The Ministry of Justice disputes the figure, saying its own review puts the number of un-monitored individuals at 5,450.

headlines.title: BBC radio DJ Paul Gambaccini diagnosed with Alzheimer's
headlines.description: In a statement, the presenter said he would continue to present his programmes on BBC Radio 2 and Greatest Hits Radio.

headlines.title: UK bakes in 35C highs as heatwave to continue next week
headlines.description: On Thursday, temperatures exceeded 34C for the eighth day this year, breaking the previous record of seven days in a calendar year.

headlines.title: How much sunscreen do you need to protect your skin?
headlines.description: What to look for when choosing sunscreen and how to apply it to get the maximum protection.

headlines.title: How can I get air conditioning in my home and how much does it cost?
headlines.description: As summers in the UK get hotter, is it time for air conditioning to become a permanent feature in most homes?

headlines.title: 'People don't realise how at risk they are': A day with an ambulance service in a heatwave
headlines.description: The BBC join a crew based in Exeter as the UK sizzles in one of its longest-running heatwaves.

headlines.title: Why heatwaves hit women harder
headlines.description: Experts are calling for better awareness of the heat-related risks to women and more targeted efforts to protect them.

headlines.title: All the best looks from Paris Haute Couture Week 2026
headlines.description: Scorching temperatures in the French capital accompanied the hottest week in haute couture.

headlines.title: The youth clubs fighting to stay relevant in the social media age
headlines.description: Funding has shrunk and hundreds of youth clubs have closed. But those that remain are trying to reinvent what youth services look like.

headlines.title: Ann Widdecombe: Brexit-backing, animal-loving Strictly star
headlines.description: A leading figure on the right of British politics who reinvented herself as a reality TV star.

headlines.title: We've saved £6,000 on holidays by swapping homes with strangers
headlines.description: The BBC looks at the growing trend of people swapping homes to cut holiday costs.

headlines.title: Weekly quiz: Why did Australia's PM apologise to Kylie Minogue?
headlines.description: How much attention did you pay to what happened in the world over the past seven days?

headlines.title: How cows are helping one of Britain's rarest butterflies
headlines.description: The high brown fritillary needs bracken and a certain violet to thrive - but everything in moderation.

headlines.title: I was needle spiked in a nightclub on my first holiday with friends
headlines.description: Teenage footballer Taylor Coulter needed hospital treatment after being injected by a stranger in the Spanish resort of Magaluf.

headlines.title: England's Guehi a doubt for Norway game and Rice misses training due to sickness bug
headlines.description: England defender Marc Guehi is managing a slight hamstring injury and could miss Saturday's Norway quarter-final.

headlines.title: France cruise into semi-finals - could this be the best Les Bleus ever?
headlines.description: World Cup winners in 1998 and 2018, France have created another special team and in beating Morocco showed why they are favourites to win the 2026 competition.

headlines.title: Norway's Haaland says pressure is all on England
headlines.description: Norway striker Erling Haaland says all the pressure is on England before the two sides' World Cup quarter-final on Saturday.

headlines.title: Loyal and royal: How the future King became a football superfan
headlines.description: A love of football connects William to the country he will one day reign over in a way polo would not.

headlines.title: Vapes to have less enticing names and flavours to protect children
headlines.description: People are being consulted about plans to stop vape companies using enticing flavour descriptions that "attract" children into experimenting.

headlines.title: Labour under Burnham will be faster and bolder, says minister
headlines.description: Lisa Nandy was speaking as 322 Labour MPs voted to back Burnham, meaning he is likely to become leader.

headlines.title: Palestinians mourn Gaza World Cup screenings organiser killed in Israeli strike
headlines.description: Aid worker Mohammed al-Wahidi had become a prominent humanitarian figure during the Israel-Hamas war.

headlines.title: EasyJet agrees to surprise takeover bid as rival US firm swoops in
headlines.description: The airline says a bid from US firm Apollo has trumped a recent takeover offer from Castlelake.

headlines.title: How an app in South Korea will let victims track their stalkers
headlines.description: Officials say the app will help stalking victims, but some experts question the impact it might have.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Andy Burnham (Almost) Wins
headlines.description: Burnham on track to be next PM as 322 Labour MPs back him.

headlines.title: After 250 years, do US citizens still believe in the American Dream?
headlines.description: Historian Heather Cox Richardson on what history tells us about modern America

headlines.title: France cruise into semi-finals - could this be the best Les Bleus ever?
headlines.description: World Cup winners in 1998 and 2018, France have created another special team and in beating Morocco showed why they are favourites to win the 2026 competition.

headlines.title: England advised to avoid alcohol around matches after off-field incidents
headlines.description: England men's players are advised to avoid drinking alcohol on the day before and the day after matches, under new behaviour guidelines.

headlines.title: Guehi a doubt for Norway game and Rice ill with bug
headlines.description: England defender Marc Guehi is managing a slight hamstring injury and could miss Saturday's Norway quarter-final.

headlines.title: Mexico boss' explicit heckle a bit of fun - Gordon
headlines.description: England winger Anthony Gordon says being sworn at by Mexico boss Javier Aguirre in a viral moment of their World Cup match was "just a bit of fun".
